channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: McConnell says a fall led to his hospitalization, breaking weeks of silence
item.description: Sen. Mitch McConnell on Sunday revealed that a fall led to his hospitalization, breaking the silence about his condition after weeks of mounting speculation about the Kentucky Republican's health.
item.content:encoded:
Sen. Mitch McConnell on Sunday revealed that a fall led to his hospitalization, breaking the silence about his condition after weeks of mounting speculation about the Kentucky Republican's health.

(Image credit: Mark Humphrey)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Fire breaks out at a pub in Bangkok, killing at least 27 people, officials say
item.description: Footage shared online by first responders shows a huge blaze raging and plumes coming out of the front door of the pub in the northern part of the Thai capital.
item.content:encoded:
Footage shared online by first responders shows a huge blaze raging and plumes coming out of the front door of the pub in the northern part of the Thai capital.

(Image credit: Sakchai Lalit)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Toronto police looking for suspects after deadly shooting at festival
item.description: At least two people are dead and at least four people are injured after a shooting on Saturday night at a festival celebrating Latin culture in Canada's biggest city.
item.content:encoded:
At least two people are dead and at least four people are injured after a shooting on Saturday night at a festival celebrating Latin culture in Canada's biggest city.

(Image credit: Keito Newman)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: What to know about the cyclosporiasis outbreak hitting more than half of U.S. states
item.description: A surge of cases of the intestinal illness that causes diarrhea and nausea has been detected in 31 states, according to federal health authorities, but the source is still under investigation.
item.content:encoded:
A surge of cases of the intestinal illness that causes diarrhea and nausea has been detected in 31 states, according to federal health authorities, but the source is still under investigation.

(Image credit: Ron Harris)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: A promising tale from Senegal of fish, rice .... and dangerous worms
item.description: Farmers in Senegal are welcoming fish into their rice paddies. The hope is they'll fertilize the crop, be a source of food ... and eat the snails that carry parasitic worms.
item.content:encoded:
Farmers in Senegal are welcoming fish into their rice paddies. The hope is they'll fertilize the crop, be a source of food ... and eat the snails that carry parasitic worms.

(Image credit: Ricci Shyrock for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: Campaign text messages could soon get more effective — and annoying
item.description: Taught to sound like a candidate, bots are engaging voters with personalized text messages, making AI-generated texting conversations the latest tool political campaigns are using to connect.
item.content:encoded:
Taught to sound like a candidate, bots are engaging voters with personalized text messages, making AI-generated texting conversations the latest tool political campaigns are using to connect.

(Image credit: Charly Triballeau)
item.dc:creator: Maham Javaid

item.title: An artist brought 'I.C.E. pops' to a Texas campus. The show was shut down in days
item.description: The Trump administration's executive orders have meant that administrators are questioning what art can — and can't — be seen on campus.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration's executive orders have meant that administrators are questioning what art can — and can't — be seen on campus.

(Image credit: Laila AnnMarie Stevens for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: What a Monopoly importer learned when it tried to make things in the U.S.A.
item.description: After getting hit with tariffs for the imported board games he sells, Jonathan Silva decided to see if he could produce a version of his Monopoly game in the United States. This is what he learned.
item.content:encoded:
After getting hit with tariffs for the imported board games he sells, Jonathan Silva decided to see if he could produce a version of his Monopoly game in the United States. This is what he learned.

(Image credit: WS Game Company)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham of South Carolina, Trump ally and foreign policy hawk, dies at 71
item.description: His office said Graham died Saturday night after a "brief and sudden illness." The Republican senator was instrumental in enacting Trump's policy and staffing priorities.
item.content:encoded:
His office said Graham died Saturday night after a "brief and sudden illness." The Republican senator was instrumental in enacting Trump's policy and staffing priorities.

(Image credit: Jeffrey Collins)
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: Messi and Argentina survive another close call to reach the World Cup semifinals
item.description: Argentina was taken to the brink in its first two knockout games. In Saturday's quarterfinal against Switzerland, the Albiceleste survived again to advance to the semis, where they will face England.
item.content:encoded:
Argentina was taken to the brink in its first two knockout games. In Saturday's quarterfinal against Switzerland, the Albiceleste survived again to advance to the semis, where they will face England.

(Image credit: Odd Andersen)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan
